author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
The Most Illustrious Instance of Predestination is Christ Jesus.
title: The Most Illustrious Instance of Predestination is Christ Jesus.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 30 [XV.]—The Most Illustrious Instance of Predestination is Christ Jesus.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, the most illustrious Light of predestination and grace is the Saviour Himself,—the Mediator Himself between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And, pray, by what preceding merits of its own, whether of works or of faith, did the human nature which is in Him procure for itself that it should be this?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let this have an answer, I beg.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: That man, whence did He deserve this—to be assumed by the Word co-eternal with the Father into unity of person, and be the only-begotten Son of God?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Was it because any kind of goodness in Him preceded?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What did He do before?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What did He believe?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What did He ask, that He should attain to this unspeakable excellence?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Was it not by the act and the assumption of the Word that that man, from the time He began to be, began to be the only Son of God?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Did not that woman, full of grace, conceive the only Son of God?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Was He not born the only Son of God, of the Holy Spirit and the Virgin Mary,—not of the lust of the flesh, but by God’s peculiar gift?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Was it to be feared that as age matured this man, He would sin of free will?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Or was the will in Him not free on that account? and was it not so much the more free in proportion to the greater impossibility of His becoming the servant of sin?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Certainly, in Him human nature—that is to say, our nature—specially received all those specially admirable gifts, and any others that may most truly be said to be peculiar to Him, by virtue of no preceding merits of its own.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let a man here answer to God if he dare, and say, Why was it not I also?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And if he should hear, “O man, who art thou that repliest against God?” let him not at this point restrain himself, but increase his impudence and say, “How is it that I hear, Who art thou, O man? since I am what I hear,—that is, a man, and He of whom I speak is but the same?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Why should not I also be what He is?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For it is by grace that He is such and so great; why is grace different when nature is common?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Assuredly, there is no respect of persons with God.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: I say, not what Christian man, but what madman will say this?
